name: Calydonian Boar

visual_attributes.size_and_scale: gigantic, larger than a typical wild boar
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: muscular neck and body, with prominent tusks

domain_specific_visual.mythological_aura: emanates a fiery and fearsome presence
domain_specific_visual.environmental_impact: scorched earth and destroyed vegetation in its path

domain_specific_functional.role_in_myth: central figure in the Calydonian Boar Hunt
domain_specific_functional.impact_on_heroes: caused division and conflict among hunters

relationships.description: Sent by Artemis to punish King Oeneus for neglecting her in his offerings.
Meleager led the hunt that ultimately killed the boar.
Atalanta was the first to wound the boar, leading to its eventual death.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents divine punishment and the gathering of heroes

contrastive_constraints: NOT a creature of benign or neutral intent
